reasoning: The statistical analysis from the FRED tool estimates a baseline probability of around 45% for an increase by November 16, largely due to recent high volatility and historical seasonal patterns that suggest moderation or decline heading into the end of the year. The primary credit value recently jumped quite a bit in late September and October, and seems to be returning to a lower average, which supports a lower probability of further increases. We anchor to the FRED tool estimate of 0.45 for the initial resolution date, and gradually pull probabilities closer to 0.50 (0.47 and 0.49) for subsequent dates (December 2025 and February 2026) to reflect increasing uncertainty.
